data.title: Trump demands Microsoft fire its president of global affairs over national security issues
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump has demanded Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) fire Lisa Monaco, its president of global affairs, deeming she is a "menace to U.S. national security."

Microsoft hired Monaco for the role in June. She previously served as deputy attorney general in the Biden Administration and as homeland security advisor during the Obama Administration.

"Monaco has been shockingly hired as the President of Global Affairs for Microsoft, in a very senior role with access to Highly Sensitive Information," Trump said in a post on Truth Social on Friday afternoon. "Monaco's having that kind of access is unacceptable, and cannot be allowed to stand. She is a menace to U.S. National Security, especially given the major contracts that Microsoft has with the United States Government."

Trump accused Monaco of being involved in "deep state conspiracies," such as "RUSSIA, RUSSIA, RUSSIA, the January 6th Hoax, the Illegal Raid on Mar-a-Lago, the Biden 'Autopen' Scandal, the Documents Witch Hunt, and more!"

He said she recently lost all federal security clearances and has been banned from all federal properties.

"It is my opinion that Microsoft should immediately terminate the employment of Lisa Monaco," he added. 

In March, the Trump Administration rescinded security clearances and access to classified information on a number of individuals, including Monaco, Antony Blinken, Jacob Sullivan, Mark Zaid, Norman Eisen, Letitia James, Alvin Bragg, Andrew Weissmann, Hillary Clinton, Elizabeth Cheney, Kamala Harris, Adam Kinzinger, Fiona Hill, Alexander Vindman, Joseph R. Biden Jr., and any other member of Joseph R. Biden Jr.'s family.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Big 5 Sporting Goods to go private by Worldwide Golf Group acquisition
data.description:
• Big 5 Sporting Goods (NASDAQ:BGFV) stockholders have approved its acquisition by WSG Merger, a subsidiary of Worldwide Golf Group.

  • The transaction is expected to close on or around September 30, 2025.

  • Following the acquisition, Big 5 will become a private company, and its common stock will be delisted from public markets.

  • Source: Press release

data.title: Gold racks up sixth straight weekly gain; silver surges to cap strongest week in 14 years
data.description:
Gold futures rose Friday after a key U.S. inflation data point met market expectations, reinforcing expectations that the Federal Reserve will roll out further interest rate cuts this year, while silver continued a rally that resulted in its strongest week since October 2011.

The U.S. personal consumption expenditures price index rose 0.3% in August, in line with estimates and following a 0.2% gain in July, and annual headline PCE inflation rose to 2.7%, also in line but the highest in six months, from 2.6% in the July report.

The University of Michigan's final reading of its consumer sentiment survey for September came in at 55.1, lower than the preliminary reading of 55.4 and well below August’s final reading of 58.2.

Investors now see an 88% probability of a rate cut in October and a 65% chance of another cut in December, according to the CME FedWatch Tool.

Meanwhile, silver and platinum are gaining momentum, with investors turning to more affordable alternatives as gold breaks records on a regular basis.

"The price of gold may have recently reached a new record high, but the price of silver has actually performed even more impressively," Commerzbank head of commodity strategy Thu Lan Nguyen said in a note, as silver has surged 59% so far this year, outperforming gold, which has gained 43%. "The record high price of gold is now deterring many and... alternatives are therefore being sought."

Industrial demand also has been a factor underpinning strength in silver, with an extra boost this week from China President Xi's pledge to cut his country's net carbon emissions 7%-10% by 2035; silver is a key component in solar cells.

Platinum jumped 2.5% on Friday to $1,568.21, and has surged 71% YTD to its highest level since 2013, partly on expectations of another annual supply deficit.

Comex gold and silver both scored their sixth consecutive weekly gain, with the front-month September gold contract (XAUUSD:CUR) closing +2.8% to $3,775.30/oz, its second-highest settlement in history, and front-month September silver (XAGUSD:CUR) ending +8.6% to $46.221/oz, its best settlement value since April 29, 2011.

On Friday, the gold and silver front-month gained 1% and 3.4%, respectively.

data.title: Northann Corp. registers 120M shares for resale
data.description:
• Northann Corp (NYSE:NCL) has filed a prospectus to allow the resale of up to 120M common shares by existing investors.
  • The registered shares relate to two prior share purchase agreements: 40M shares issued on March 31, 2025 under the Oneflow SPA, split equally among 5 investors (8M each) and 80M shares issued on September 3, 2025 under the X29 SPA, also split equally among 5 investors (16M each).
  • Northann will not receive any proceeds from the resale of these shares.
  • Source: SEC filing

data.title: Biodexa Pharmaceuticals files $100M shelf for flexible securities offerings
data.description:
• Biodexa Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:BDRX) has filed a $100M shelf registration for future offerings of various securities.
  • Securities may include ordinary shares ADS representing ordinary shares debt securities, warrants, rights, and units.
  • Source: SEC filing

data.title: SKYX Platforms registers 5.5M shares for resale
data.description:
• SKYX Platforms (NASDAQ:SKYX) filed a prospectus to allow resale of up to 5.52M shares of common stock by certain selling securityholders.
  • Includes 5.5M shares issuable upon conversion of a $6M subordinated secured convertible note issued on September 2, 2025, convertible at $1.20/share and 19,840 shares issued or issuable via private placement.
  • SKYX will not receive any proceeds from resale.
  • Source: SEC filing

data.title: Bolt Projects registers 20M shares for potential sale under $20M equity line
data.description:
• Bolt Projects Holdings (NASDAQ:BSLK) filed a prospectus covering up to 20.09M shares of common stock for resale by Ascent Partners Fund.
  • Includes: 20M purchase shares available under a $20M equity line of credit with Ascent and 85,588 commitment shares already issued as consideration for Ascent’s commitment.
  • Bolt will not receive proceeds from Ascent’s resale of shares, but may raise up to $20M in gross proceeds through future sales to Ascent under the September 12, 2025 purchase agreement.
  • Source: SEC filing

data.title: 37 Capital announces non brokered private placement financing, grants options
data.description:
• 37 Capital (OTCPK:HHHEF) has  proposed to conduct a non-brokered private placement to raise up to $375,000 by the issuance of up to 3M units of the company at a price of $0.125 per unit.
  • Each unit will consist of one common share of the company and one share purchase warrant to acquire one common share of the company at a price of $0.15 per share for a period of three years.
  • The company also announced the granting of a total of 2.06M incentive stock options to directors and officers exercisable at the price of $0.12 per common share for a period of three years.
  • The funds raised from the financing will be used towards general working capital.

data.title: Nexstar to reinstate Jimmy Kimmel's show on ABC stations
data.description:
Nexstar Media Group (NASDAQ:NXST) on Friday said “Jimmy Kimmel Live!” will return to the company’s owned and partner television stations affiliated with the ABC Television Network.

This comes just three days after media and entertainment giant Disney (DIS) revoked the comedian's suspension following his remarks on slain political activist Charlie Kirk.

“We have had discussions with executives at The Walt Disney Company and appreciate their constructive approach to addressing our concerns,” Nexstar said.

Earlier in the day, broadcaster Sinclair (SBGI) also reinstated the talk show.

Sinclair owns more ABC-affiliated stations in the U.S., totaling about 40, while Nexstar, being the largest local TV station in the country, owns about 30.

Kimmel's suspension lasted only for six days after advocates from both sides of the political aisle disagreed with the move. Disney also witnessed a wave of subscription cancellations by users who viewed the action against the comedian to be harsh and as an attack on free speech.

data.title: Spirit Airlines cutting 40 routes, hires Amazon exec to help with network planning
data.description:
As part of its ongoing efforts to slash costs and remain operational during bankruptcy, Spirit Airlines, the airline operating under Spirit Aviation (OTC:FLYYQ), will suspend about 40 routes, according to a memo to employees viewed by CNBC.

The suspended routes are expected to come primarily out of Hartford, Connecticut, and Minneapolis, and are part of Spirit’s plan to trim its routes by 25% in November. This prompted the company to recently furlough 1,800 flight attendants.

“While the news has been tough, we believe the clarity will help us move forward as a team,” Rana Ghosh, Spirit’s chief commercial officer, said in a note to employees cited by CNBC.

The company also tapped former Amazon executive Andrea Lusso to help with network planning. 

Late last month, the troubled carrier declared bankruptcy for the second time in a year after its last restructuring failed to put the carrier on a sustainable path.

Just weeks prior to filing Chapter 11 protection and only five months after emerging from its first bankruptcy restructuring, Spirit issued a “going concern” warning as it could not maintain enough liquidity as required by creditors. The carrier also blamed increased domestic capacity in the low-fare space and adverse macroeconomic conditions for its financial challenges.

Over the past month alone, Spirit Aviation (OTC:FLYYQ) shares have lost 74% in value and have traded under $1 per share for 18 consecutive days.
